definition: The Treasury basis trade ($800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage) creates a novel cross-market contagion channel: unwind triggers Treasury selling → swap spread widening → higher funding costs → reduced dealer liquidity → wider credit bid-asks → forced selling by levered credit funds → VIX spike → equity risk-parity deleveraging. Fundamentally alters the flight-to-quality dynamic by converting the Treasury market from safe haven to contagion source.
evidence: Partially validated by September 2019 repo crisis and March 2020 Treasury dysfunction
Treasury market becomes contagion source rather than safe haven
Identified as rates_strategist_01's strongest unique contribution in pair_3 debate
March 2020 partially validated mechanism ($1.6T Fed emergency intervention required)
Dealer inventory decline from ~$250B to ~$30-40B impairs shock absorption
Post-2020 regulatory attention may have reduced risk
SEC central clearing mandate Q2 2026 may force position adjustment
open_questions: What is the current size and leverage of the basis trade?
Has post-2020 regulatory attention reduced the risk?
Could cross-margining requirements trigger credit margin calls from Treasury unwinds?
How much has post-2020 regulatory attention actually reduced the risk?
What are the exact size and leverage estimates for current basis trade positions?
Will the SEC central clearing mandate cause orderly adjustment or disruptive unwind?
